title: centaurium scilloides subsp. massonii
intro: Centaurium scilloides subsp. massonii, also known as Mountain Centaury or Mountain Centaur, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Gentian family, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of glycosides and iridoids. The health benefits of Centaurium scilloides subsp. massonii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and act as an expectorant, making it useful in treating conditions such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to soothe respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
